heading: Recovered Sixto stage-angle schedule is supported by the printed angle packet, while the timing-graph tie remains structural only
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto, angle-schedule, angle-packet, timing-graph]
body: Built `sixto_angle_schedule_bridge.json` and `build_sixto_angle_schedule_bridge.py` to test whether the recovered internal Sixto stage-angle schedule `55.96339° -> 31.035109° -> 16.059987°` ties back to the printed angle packet or to the timing graph. The printed-angle bridge is supported: stage 2 is within `0.42009°` of the printed orange angle `55.5433°`, stage 4 is within `1.099583°` of the printed green angle `17.15957°`, and stage 3 is within `0.162805°` of the geometric mean `sqrt(theta_orange * theta_green) = 30.872304°`, while it misses the arithmetic midpoint by `5.316326°`. The timing-graph link is still not numeric. The same witness does contain the top-right `X0..X3 / Y1..Y3` graph and the bottom stage chain, but no graph-curve extraction has been done yet, so that part remains structural co-presence only. Honest verdict: the visible stage-angle schedule is now genuinely tied to the printed angle packet, not yet to the timing graph.
